description: Professional TTS voice generation prompt template for synthetic voice models (ElevenLabs, Bark, etc.)
dimensions: voice_profile: gender, age range, accent/dialect, vocal weight (light / medium / full)
mic_technique: close-mic / mid, breath audibility (silent / subtle / natural)
room_treatment: dry / slight room — TTS models respond to acoustic descriptors
emotional_tone: primary emotion the voice must carry
pace: exact WPM, rhythm feel, pause placement strategy
delivery_style: corporate / conversational / instructional / storytelling / energetic
emphasis_strategy: which word types to stress (nouns / verbs / numbers), rising vs falling inflection
context: where and how the audio will be used (app UI, e-learning, IVR, podcast intro…)
post_processing: EQ character, compression, any spatial treatment
examples: Female voice, mid-30s, neutral French accent, medium vocal weight. Close-mic'd, breath nearly silent, dry studio. Warm confidence — the tone of a knowledgeable colleague, not a corporate robot. Steady 128 wpm, natural micro-pauses after commas, full stop pauses of ~0.6s. Stress falls on action verbs and key nouns; gentle rising inflection on questions. Soft sibilants, no harshness above 8 kHz. Used for e-learning module introductions on a SaaS platform. Post: low-mid warmth at 300 Hz (+1.5 dB), gentle de-essing at 7 kHz, 4:1 compression with 10ms attack.

Male voice, late-20s, neutral American accent, light-medium vocal weight. Close-mic'd, subtle natural breath, dry. Energetic and approachable — think startup product demo. Brisk 145 wpm with punchy delivery on feature names, deliberate 0.8s pause before key CTAs. Hard consonants on technical terms, crisp sibilants. Used for in-app onboarding tooltips. Post: high-pass at 100 Hz, presence boost at 2.5 kHz (+2 dB), fast-attack limiting.
